headline: DEVIATIONS THAT SURVIVE INSPECTION
subheading: Risk-based triage, defensible root cause and CAPA that actually closes for UK and EU teams.
flux_prompt: An abstract editorial composition evoking pharmaceutical deviation investigation and quality control. A deep navy background dissolves into a luminous teal and cyan flow diagram of interconnected nodes and arrows, suggesting a five-stage process lifecycle from capture through root-cause analysis to resolution. At the centre, a glowing magnified focal point examines a single anomalous element diverging from an orderly grid of identical glass vials, a visual metaphor for a deviation departing from specification. Faint stylised fishbone and branching fault-tree lines radiate outward as elegant translucent ribbons, while a subtle checklist-and-tick motif resolves into a clean checkmark of closure. Soft holographic data layers, audit-trail timelines and precise concentric rings convey scientific rigour and documented control. A single fresh-green accent highlights the moment of approved closure, lifting the cool clinical palette. Polished, premium, calm and authoritative, with shallow depth of field and gentle volumetric light. 1500x900 composition, 5:3 aspect ratio, professional editorial illustration, premium quality, clean composition, modern visual storytelling, professional lighting, no text, no watermark, no logo, ultra detailed
